title: RequestClosed
description: Published by Dispatch Core when a request reaches a terminal FSM state, successful or not. This is the final event in a request's lifecycle and the one Evaluation waits for to compute per-request KPIs.

event_id.description: Unique ID for this specific event instance (not the request).

request_id.description: Correlation ID shared by every event belonging to the same patient request, from submission to closure. This is how Evaluation reconstructs a full request lifecycle across services.

version.description: Schema version for this event type, e.g. '1.0'. Bump on breaking changes so consumers can branch on it during migration.

produced_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the producing service emitted this event.

producer.description: Name of the service that published this event, e.g. 'ingestion', 'nlp-triage'. Useful for debugging and for Evaluation's traceability.

pro_id.description: Professional ultimately assigned, if outcome is 'dispatched'; null otherwise.

ttfa_seconds.description: Final time-to-first-assignment for this request, for KPI aggregation.

total_refusals.description: Total number of professionals who refused/timed-out before closure.
